description: Authoritative format contract for the four execution identity types used across the local-first AI platform. All format strings reflect actual observed repo behavior. Types are mutually exclusive by definition and must never be used interchangeably.

job_id.description: Identifies a single discrete execution unit — one scheduled or dispatched job lifecycle. Scoped to framework/job_schema.py Job and framework/canonical_job_schema.py CanonicalJob. Must not be used as session_id, telemetry_run_id, or plan_id.

session_id.description: Groups one or more jobs sharing an intent boundary. Carried by CanonicalSession and propagated into CanonicalJob. Must not be used as job_id, telemetry_run_id, or plan_id. Observed patterns include descriptive slug form and full UUID form depending on call site; the control-plane canonical form is a descriptive slug.
session_id.fallback_derivation: Some call sites derive session_id as '{parent_session_id}-{suffix}' when a sub-session is scoped to a parent (observed in framework/runtime_validation_pack.py).

telemetry_run_id.description: Identifies one contiguous telemetry bundle produced by a Phase 1 runtime invocation. Carried by RunBundleManifest.run_id in framework/runtime_telemetry_schema.py. Distinct from job_id (no 'job-' prefix) and from session_id (no intent grouping semantics). Must not be used as job_id, session_id, or plan_id.

plan_id.description: Identifies one execution route plan or orchestration plan produced by a manager or planner surface. Carried by framework/execution_route_plan_schema.py::ExecutionRoutePlan.plan_id and used in manager trace records. Must not be used as job_id, session_id, or telemetry_run_id. Format is context-derived: typically '{context-prefix}-{timestamp-or-slug}' or '{parent_id}-{op-index}'.

invariants: job_id, session_id, telemetry_run_id, and plan_id are mutually exclusive identity scopes.
A single execution context may carry all four simultaneously; each refers to a different scope.
job_id prefix 'job-' is reserved exclusively for job_id values.
telemetry_run_id prefix 'run-' is reserved exclusively for telemetry_run_id values.
No two identity types may share the same value in a well-formed execution context.
